name: gitlab
description: GitLab operations via glab CLI. Use when user mentions: MR, merge request, gitlab issue, pipeline, CI status, glab, or when git remote shows gitlab.com or self-hosted GitLab.
capabilities: Create, view, and manage merge requests with full context
Create, list, and close GitLab issues from terminal
Check CI/CD pipeline status and view build logs
Authenticate with GitLab using glab auth login
Clone and view repositories via glab CLI
author: ETeissonniere
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only prompt guidelines and glab command references. Static findings are false positives: SHA-256 hashes flagged as weak crypto (SHA-256 is secure), 'sudo' in package installation docs flagged as privilege escalation (legitimate apt install command), content hashes flagged as C2 keywords (standard Git metadata), backtick notation flagged as shell execution (standard markdown documentation), and git remote detection flagged as reconnaissance (legitimate GitLab detection). No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: GitLab projects require separate CLI commands for merge requests, issues, and pipelines. This skill provides quick access to glab commands for all GitLab operations. Automate your GitLab workflow directly from Claude.
userTitle: Manage GitLab merge requests and issues
limitations: Requires glab CLI to be installed locally on your system
Requires GitLab authentication via glab auth login before use
Cannot modify GitLab project settings directly through this skill
Self-hosted GitLab instances require prior glab configuration

useCases.title: Monitor CI/CD pipelines
useCases.description: Check pipeline status, view logs, and retry failed builds without leaving the terminal

useCases.title: Review merge requests
useCases.description: List, view, checkout, and approve merge requests with full comment context

useCases.title: Track GitLab issues
useCases.description: Create issues, track assignments, and manage issue discussions from command line

promptTemplates.title: List open merge requests
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all open merge requests for this repository

promptTemplates.title: Create merge request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a merge request with title 'Feature: Add user authentication' and description 'This PR adds OAuth login functionality'

promptTemplates.title: Check pipeline status
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the current CI/CD pipeline status? Show me the latest build logs

promptTemplates.title: Manage issues
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new issue for the bug we found in the payment module, then list all issues assigned to me

bestPractices: Always verify glab authentication with 'glab auth status' before operations
Use '--comments' flag when viewing MRs or issues for complete context
Check 'glab ci status' before running new pipelines to avoid duplicate builds
antiPatterns: Running glab commands without first confirming authentication
Creating merge requests without checking for uncommitted local changes
Using pipeline commands on non-GitLab repositories

faq.question: How do I install glab CLI?
faq.answer: Install via Homebrew (brew install glab), apt (sudo apt install glab), or download from GitHub releases.

faq.question: Does this work with self-hosted GitLab?
faq.answer: Yes. Configure your GitLab instance with 'glab config set gitlab.com your-gitlab-url'.

faq.question: What permissions does glab need?
faq.answer: glab requires a GitLab API token with appropriate scopes like read_api or api depending on operations.

faq.question: Is my data sent to third parties?
faq.answer: glab communicates directly with your configured GitLab instance only. No external services involved.

faq.question: Why are commands not working?
faq.answer: Common issues: not authenticated (run glab auth status), wrong directory, or glab not installed.

faq.question: How is this different from git CLI?
faq.answer: git handles version control locally. glab provides GitLab-specific MRs, issues, and pipelines.
